description: A 'weloni' is a being's physical whole. Its parts move and sense; the whole body can rest, hurt, or receive care. It remains the body when discussed after death. 'miona' (person) is a human participant, 'lioru' (life) the living condition, and 'lima' (alive) the corresponding quality.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then move forward and unround into middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for voiced 'l' and continue into stressed middle-height back rounded 'o'. Place the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with high-front unrounded 'i'.
usage_notes: Possessor-first order identifies the body being discussed, as in 'lopia weloni' (the child's body). The grammar states a relation rather than ownership of a person.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Mindfulness can begin with this whole body before attention moves to one sensation, posture, or part.

semantic_domains.physical: the complete physical form of a living or once-living being
semantic_domains.wisdom: the whole body as an object of sustained contemplative attention
